description: Advanced marine inspection integrating SAR systems, multi-agent underwater-surface coordination, and persistent Edge AI over low-bandwidth satellite links.
learning_objectives: Understand marine and coastal ecosystems: coral reefs, seagrass beds, mangroves, beaches, and marine protected areas (MPAs)
Build and train AI models for marine wildlife detection (turtles, dolphins, whales, sharks), species classification, and counting
Create geospatial and time‑series data pipelines for tracking habitat change, erosion, and illegal fishing activities
Deploy optimized models on edge hardware for real‑time wildlife detection and vessel monitoring during over‑water flights
Implement autonomous flight patterns for coastal surveys, transect lines, and reef mapping
Design fail‑safe mechanisms for over‑water operations (auto‑return, flotation, corrosion resistance)
Comply with marine protection regulations, aviation restrictions, and wildlife interaction guidelines

modules.title: SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) Image Processing
modules.description: Exploit radar backscatter data to track coastal erosion, ship wakes, and oil spills through heavy cloud cover.
topics: Interferometric SAR (InSAR) phase unwrapping
Speckle Noise Reduction algorithms (Lee, Frost, Kuan filters)
Vessel anomaly detection via AIS and SAR mismatching
Coastal subsidence monitoring with time-series SAR
Deploying real-time SAR processors via Triton

modules.title: Bathymetric Green Lidar Classification
modules.description: Process point clouds mapping shallow coastal waters and reef structures.
topics: Water column correction & depth normalization
Benthic habitat mapping with 3D CNNs (PointNet++)
Combining Topo-bathy Lidar with coastal drone feeds
Extracting accurate Digital Elevation Models (DEM)
Identifying rogue wave signatures in Lidar point clouds

modules.title: Federated Learning over Low Bandwidth
modules.description: Operating models in disconnected, intermittent maritime environments.
topics: Knowledge Distillation on Edge Devices
Delta Model Updates over LoRa/Iridium Satellite networks
Federated Optimization strategies (FedAvg, SCAFFOLD)
DDS QoS Profiles for lossy maritime links
Edge caching & synchronization logic

modules.title: Heterogeneous Multi-Domain Swarms
modules.description: Coordinate Aerial Drones, Surface Vessels (USV), and Underwater Vehicles (UUV) for unified missions.
topics: Cross-domain sensor fusion (Acoustic + Optical)
Auction-based Task Allocation for autonomous agents
Underwater Acoustic Modem delay handling
Shared World Modeling in Marine Environments
Autonomous landing on moving platforms (RTK + ArUco)

capstone_project.name: Autonomous Marine Wildlife and Coastal Health Drone
capstone_project.description: Build and test a drone system that autonomously surveys a designated coastal segment (simulated in HIL) to count sea turtles (or another marine species) and assess shoreline condition (erosion, debris). The system must fly a predetermined transect, detect and classify at least two species (simulated), compute counts with >85% accuracy, and identify areas of erosion or debris accumulation. Generate a report with geotagged detections and maps. Mission duration < 20 minutes for a 2 km coastline.
deliverables: Trained wildlife detection and habitat classification models (quantized, <150 MB)
ROS 2 nodes for survey planning, detection, and logging
PostGIS database for sightings and shoreline data
HIL simulation report with detection accuracy and mission completion time
Safety plan including wildlife approach distances and over‑water procedures

resources.name: NOAA Coral Reef Conservation Program
resources.description: Guidelines and datasets for coral monitoring

resources.name: Marine Mammal Protection Act (MMPA)
resources.description: Legal framework for marine wildlife protection

glossary.definition: Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar. A technique that uses two or more SAR images to generate maps of surface deformation or digital elevation.
A granular interference that degrades the quality of active radar, SAR, and ultrasound images.
Relating to the ecological region at the lowest level of a body of water such as an ocean or a lake.
The process of transferring knowledge from a large, complex neural network (teacher) to a smaller, simpler model (student).
Long Range. A proprietary low-power wide-area network modulation technique.
